title: Lowe's Cuts Sales Forecast as Consumers Decide Against DIY
text: Consumers are putting do-it-yourself (DIY) projects on hold, which is bad news for Lowe's. title: LOWE'S REPORTS THIRD QUARTER 2023 SALES AND EARNINGS RESULTS
text: — Comparable Sales Decreased 7.4%; Diluted EPS of $3.06 — — Updates Full Year 2023 Outlook — MOORESVILLE, N.C. , Nov. 21, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE: LOW) today reported net earnings of $1.8 billion and diluted earnings per share (EPS) of $3.06 for the quarter ended Nov. 3, 2023, compared to diluted EPS of $0.25 in the third quarter of 2022, which included a pre-tax long-lived asset impairment of $2.1 billion related to its Canadian retail business.

title: Lowe's Is An Attractive Addition To Your Portfolio

The company has experienced significant revenue and EPS growth, driven by organic growth and cost reduction measures. title: Lowe's: Going 'All-In' At This Valuation
text: With mortgage rates hitting a two-decade high and putting pressure on existing home sales, Lowe's stock price has remained stagnant over the last two years. Lowe's, as a high-quality business, is well-positioned to weather sales slowdowns and is poised to thrive as mortgage rates decline, reigniting DIY home improvement and PRO projects. With elevated interest rates and prevailing uncertainty, Lowe's is currently trading at a discount of at least 20% compared to its historical averages, providing a significant margin of safety.
